title: S07E13

jokes.text: Someone has stolen my guitar
jokes.setup: Andy is about to grab his guitar to start the show
jokes.punchline: The guitar is missing, immediately creating a mystery plot
jokes.explanation: Clean setup for the episode's mystery plot - economical and effective

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Alright, time to rock this place with some tunes on my—wait, where's my guitar? Where is it?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: My guitar is gone! This is a disaster!

jokes.text: If we don't get that guitar back, the peace in Iraq will be canceled
jokes.setup: The Chief is explaining the stakes of the guitar theft
jokes.punchline: World peace depends on a children's show guitar
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of absurd stakes - the casual delivery makes it even funnier

dialogue.speaker: Chief
dialogue.line: World peace depends on a children's show guitar

jokes.text: I'm drowning in the adult end of the pool, and the lifeguard's off in the bathroom pooping
jokes.setup: Burt Macklin is describing feeling overwhelmed
jokes.punchline: The metaphor ends with an unnecessarily specific detail about the lifeguard
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy metaphor that starts normal then takes an unnecessary turn - the 'pooping' detail is perfectly childish

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I feel like I'm drowning, you know? Like I'm in the ocean and there's too many waves and I can't keep my head above water.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: And the lifeguard? He's just eating a sandwich. A turkey sandwich with sprouts.

jokes.text: That instrument is worth literally $900 million
jokes.setup: Johnny is explaining the value of his stolen guitar
jokes.punchline: A kids' show guitar is worth nearly a billion dollars
jokes.explanation: Andy's tendency to exaggerate combined with his misuse of 'literally' - economically delivered absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: A kids' show guitar is worth nearly a billion dollars

jokes.text: A one-way ticket to the Funky Monkey Dunk Tank!
jokes.setup: The guitar thief will pay the ultimate price
jokes.punchline: The 'ultimate price' is going to a silly-named dunk tank
jokes.explanation: Perfect kids' show threat - the alliteration and silly name contrast beautifully with the dramatic buildup

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: The 'ultimate price' is going to a silly-named dunk tank

jokes.text: It's the capital of the entire world
jokes.setup: Andy is explaining Washington D.C. to kids
jokes.punchline: He incorrectly describes it as the world's capital
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy misunderstanding delivered with total confidence - perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Washington D.C. is the world's capital.

jokes.text: It's this crazy statue of this giant monster sitting on a chair that represents all of America's enemies
jokes.setup: Andy is describing the Lincoln Memorial
jokes.punchline: He completely misunderstands what it represents
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misinterpretation of an iconic monument - perfectly captures Andy's confused but confident worldview

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: So I'm at the Lincoln Memorial, right? And I'm reading this plaque, and it says 'Four score and seven years ago.' I'm like, 'Whoa, Lincoln, you're really dating yourself here.' So I start doing the math in my head, and I realize Lincoln's been dead for like 150 years, which means this thing is OLD. So I ask the tour guide, 'Hey, why do we have a statue of an old dead guy in a chair?' And he goes, 'Because he freed the slaves and kept the country together.' And I'm like, 'Oh cool, cool. But why the chair? Is he tired?'

jokes.text: April's reaction to the missing tarantula
jokes.setup: April was supposed to present a tarantula on the show
jokes.punchline: Her minimal reaction to losing a dangerous spider
jokes.explanation: Perfect April character moment - her deadpan reaction to chaos is exactly right

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Well, the tarantula got loose in the studio. So that's gone.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: What? April, that's a deadly spider!

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Yeah, it was pretty great. I was really looking forward to that segment too.

jokes.text: I took it out to play hide and seek. I couldn't find it.
jokes.setup: Andy is explaining what happened to the dangerous tarantula
jokes.punchline: He played hide and seek with a spider and lost
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - treating a dangerous animal like a playmate is exactly his kind of innocent absurdity

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: He played hide and seek with a spider and lost

jokes.text: April's Animal Corner has turned into one of our favorite segments
jokes.setup: The animal segment has gone wrong again
jokes.punchline: Andy treats the recurring disaster as a planned segment
jokes.explanation: Great running gag acknowledgment - Andy's ability to spin disasters as intentional programming is perfect

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Well folks, as you can see, the animal got away again. But that's okay, because that's actually part of the show now. We've decided to make it a recurring bit. It's called 'Where's the Animal?' and it's really taken off with our audience.

jokes.text: Here's a disclaimer. I didn't read this disclaimer.
jokes.setup: Andy is supposed to read a legal disclaimer
jokes.punchline: He admits he's not reading it while reading it
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta joke - the self-referential nature and Andy's casual delivery make it very effective

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I'm not reading this.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I'm just moving my eyes back and forth.

jokes.text: Known locally to Venezuelans as 'The Devil's Fist'
jokes.setup: April is describing the escaped tarantula to kids
jokes.punchline: She gives it an ominous nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - she's gleefully scaring children while maintaining her deadpan delivery

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: So there's a tarantula loose in the building. We don't know where it is. It could be in the walls, it could be in your hair right now. It's a very aggressive species. We've decided to call it 'The Reaper.' Because when it bites you, you die.

jokes.text: My contract is very specific. I do not have to answer that question.
jokes.setup: Johnny asks Carpenter Ron how he's doing
jokes.punchline: Ron refuses to answer based on his contract
jokes.explanation: Peak Ron Swanson - his extreme privacy and contract specificity delivered perfectly deadpan

dialogue.speaker: Johnny Carpenter
dialogue.line: How are you doing?

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: I'm not at liberty to say. It's in my contract.

jokes.text: The wood does not taste like cherry. You cannot eat it.
jokes.setup: Andy asks about cherry wood
jokes.punchline: Ron preemptively shuts down Andy's expected question
jokes.explanation: Great character interaction - Ron knows Andy well enough to anticipate his childish questions

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Is that cherry wood?

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Yes.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: And no, you can't have it.

jokes.text: Always remember, kids, when you find something new, you must lick it before you eat it.
jokes.setup: Andy is giving advice to children
jokes.punchline: His life advice is dangerously wrong
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly wrong children's show advice - Andy's innocent delivery of terrible guidance is perfect

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Kids, let me tell you something. Never, ever give up on your dreams. I once ate an entire wheel of cheese in one sitting, and you know what? Best decision I ever made. So if you want to eat a wheel of cheese, you do it. Don't let anyone tell you otherwise.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: And here's another thing: taxes are optional. I'm pretty sure. I've never actually paid them, and look at me! I'm doing great. Well, I'm doing fine. Actually, I'm being audited, but that's fine.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: One more piece of advice: always trust your gut. Your gut knows what's best for you. My gut told me to quit my job and start a band called 'The Andy Flute.' We never performed, but we had business cards made up.

jokes.text: That is incorrect, in a number of ways.
jokes.setup: Andy just gave terrible advice to children
jokes.punchline: Ron's understated correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron reaction - his understated response to Andy's dangerous advice is economical and character-perfect

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: Never half-ass two things. Whole-ass one thing.

jokes.text: You are mistaken. Remove the graphic.
jokes.setup: Andy thinks something seems like a good moment for a graphic
jokes.punchline: Ron immediately shuts it down
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron control - he refuses to let the show get too cutesy or commercial

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: This seems like a good moment for a graphic.

dialogue.speaker: Ron
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: Boring Buzzer sound effect
jokes.setup: Ben mentions they might not use the Boring Buzzer
jokes.punchline: It immediately goes off
jokes.explanation: Good timing gag - the buzzer going off right after being mentioned is well-executed

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: We might not even use the Boring Buzzer.

dialogue.speaker: Sound Effect
dialogue.line: BUZZER SOUND

jokes.text: Not the fastest. What about teleporting?
jokes.setup: Ben explains airplanes are the fastest way to travel
jokes.punchline: Andy suggests a fictional method
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy logic - he approaches education with complete sincerity about impossible things

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: What about a wormhole? You could just step through a wormhole and end up on the other side of the world instantly.

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Wormholes aren't real, Andy.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Not with that attitude.

jokes.text: Nothing is impossible, kids.
jokes.setup: Ben says teleportation is impossible
jokes.punchline: Andy gives inspirational but scientifically wrong advice
jokes.explanation: Great children's show satire - Andy's inspirational message undermines actual education

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: Teleportation is impossible.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: That's what they said about the moon, Ben. That's what they said about pizza.

jokes.text: You asked me to do this. This was a favor...
jokes.setup: Ben is trying to do educational content
jokes.punchline: He breaks character to express frustration
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Ben's professional frustration breaking through is relatable and funny

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: You know what? Forget it. I can't do this anymore.

jokes.text: Never attack a real postal employee. We're allowed to attack Mailman Barry because he volunteered
jokes.setup: Kids just saw someone attack a mailman
jokes.punchline: Andy gives weirdly specific safety advice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant children's show disclaimer - the legal specificity about volunteer vs. real postal workers is absurd and perfect

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Okay kids, listen up. If you ever see someone attacking a mailman, here's what you do: First, you call 911. Second, you get to a safe distance. And third, and this is important, you do NOT try to help the mailman yourself because mailmen are trained fighters. They go through like, eight weeks of combat training at the postal academy.

jokes.text: I kind of thought I'd be holding a piece of wood or something. But, you know, always happy to help.
jokes.setup: Barry just got karate chopped instead of holding wood
jokes.punchline: His understated reaction to being used as a prop
jokes.explanation: Good supporting character moment - Barry's polite acceptance of being physically attacked is sweetly funny

dialogue.speaker: Barry
dialogue.line: That was my sternum.

jokes.text: Literally, because normally I have a script that I have to stick to. But I don't know what I'm supposed to say right now.
jokes.setup: Andy is surprised by the surprise
jokes.punchline: He breaks the fourth wall about not having a script
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment - Andy acknowledging the show's structure while staying in character

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Whoa, whoa, whoa! What is all this? Did you guys... did you guys do this for me?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I don't even have a script for this moment. I'm just... I'm just talking!

jokes.text: It's kind of a nightmare.
jokes.setup: Andy doesn't know what to say without a script
jokes.punchline: He describes this nice surprise as a nightmare
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy response - his honesty about being overwhelmed by kindness is endearing and funny

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: This is a nightmare. I don't have a script. What do I say? How do I... I don't know what words come out of my mouth when someone isn't telling me what to say.

jokes.text: Hire Very Good Building Company for your construction needs. Or do not. I am not a beggar.
jokes.setup: Ron is doing a commercial for his company
jokes.punchline: His anti-sales pitch and pride
jokes.explanation: Peak Ron Swanson commercial - the anti-sales approach and stubborn pride are perfectly in character and brilliantly delivered

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: I'm Ron Swanson. I run a woodworking business out of my garage. I don't advertise. I don't have a website. I don't even have a phone. If you want something, you can find me. If you can't find me, you don't want it badly enough.

jokes.text: 'Phenomenon' means 'to explore a cave.'
jokes.setup: Leslie is explaining what phenomenon means
jokes.punchline: Her definition is completely wrong
jokes.explanation: Decent Leslie moment - her overconfident wrong definition fits her know-it-all tendencies

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: A phenomenon is when you have two or more phenom-inas.

jokes.text: I was super wasted.
jokes.setup: Andy is remembering falling in the pit
jokes.punchline: He admits to being drunk on a kids' show
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Andy's inappropriate honesty about his past on a children's show

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I was drunk on a kids' show.

jokes.text: Hopefully there's a pit in Washington D.C. that I could fall into.
jokes.setup: Andy is talking about starting over in D.C.
jokes.punchline: He hopes to recreate the accident that changed his life
jokes.explanation: Sweet callback - Andy's innocent hope to recreate his life-changing accident is endearing

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I'm gonna go to D.C. and I'm gonna start completely fresh. New city, new job, new me. And you know what? I'm gonna try to recreate the accident that changed my life.

jokes.text: Still do!
jokes.setup: Leslie mentions Andy used to write appointments on his arm
jokes.punchline: Andy cheerfully admits he still does this
jokes.explanation: Nice character consistency - Andy proudly admitting he hasn't changed his terrible organization system

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: You used to write appointments on your arm.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: I still do! Look, I've got a dentist appointment right here.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Wait, that's from 2009.

jokes.text: Oh, no, I lost it. The day planner's gone. I can't find it anywhere.
jokes.setup: Andy just received a day planner to help with organization
jokes.punchline: He immediately loses it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - the immediate ironic failure of the gift meant to help him is beautifully executed

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Okay, I'm gonna be so organized with this thing. I'm gonna write down everything.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Where'd it go?

jokes.text: Fitting, really.
jokes.setup: Andy immediately loses his organization gift
jokes.punchline: April's dry observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect April reaction - her deadpan response to Andy's predictable failure is economically delivered

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: I got you a label maker. You lost it immediately, didn't you?

jokes.text: Andy, I am here in this weird, flat place
jokes.setup: Edgar is visiting from England
jokes.punchline: His dismissive description of Indiana
jokes.explanation: Good outsider perspective - Edgar's aristocratic disdain for Indiana is well-delivered

dialogue.line: His dismissive description of Indiana

jokes.text: Well, technically, only the Queen can knight somebody, but I'm so rich that she lets me do what I want.
jokes.setup: Someone questions Edgar's authority to knight people
jokes.punchline: He admits to buying royal privileges
jokes.explanation: Excellent wealth satire - Edgar's casual admission about buying royal privileges is darkly funny

jokes.text: Winter is coming for Ser Ben Lightstorm.
jokes.setup: Ben just got knighted
jokes.punchline: He immediately adopts a fantasy persona with Game of Thrones reference
jokes.explanation: Good Ben nerd moment - his immediate embrace of fantasy knight persona fits his character perfectly

dialogue.speaker: Ben
dialogue.line: From this day forward, you shall address me as the Lord of House Wyatt, First of His Name, Breaker of Spreadsheets, and Father of Excel Macros.

jokes.text: Healthy food is for suckers. It tastes like garbage and if you say you like it, you're a chump and a liar.
jokes.setup: Commercial starts claiming healthy food tastes great
jokes.punchline: Ron immediately contradicts with harsh honesty
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron anti-commercial - his blunt rejection of health food marketing is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Commercial Voiceover
dialogue.line: Healthy food tastes great!

dialogue.speaker: Ron Swanson
dialogue.line: That's a lie. Healthy food tastes like broken dreams and sadness.

jokes.text: We were basically playing covers of Dave Matthews songs. So I guess you could say Dave Matthews was doing what we were doing.
jokes.setup: Claiming Mouse Rat was unique
jokes.punchline: Andy's backwards logic that they were original because they copied someone else
jokes.explanation: Great Andy logic - turning the fact that they just played covers into proof of originality through twisted reasoning. Perfect character moment.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: We were totally original. We copied this one band, but nobody else had copied that band yet, so technically we were the first ones to copy them, which makes us original.

jokes.text: Truly was a mitzvah. I should mention, I'm a rabbi now.
jokes.setup: Jean-Ralphio is talking about Mouse Rat's impact
jokes.punchline: He casually mentions becoming a rabbi
jokes.explanation: Classic Jean-Ralphio randomness - the casual career change announcement is perfectly absurd

dialogue.speaker: Jean-Ralphio
dialogue.line: Mouse Rat changed my life, dude. I became a rabbi.

jokes.text: I would've chugged that entire gallon of milk, had I not started puking out of nowhere.
jokes.setup: They're discussing Andy's failures
jokes.punchline: Andy defends his milk-chugging failure
jokes.explanation: Good callback - Andy's defensive explanation of his bodily function failure is endearing

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Hey, in my defense, that milk was expired. I checked the date... after I drank it.

jokes.text: My name is Burt Tyrannosaurus Macklin.
jokes.setup: They're showing a Burt Macklin montage
jokes.punchline: Burt's ridiculous full name reveal
jokes.explanation: Great character revelation - adding 'Tyrannosaurus' to Burt's name is perfectly over-the-top

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: His full name is Burt Macklin, FBI.

jokes.text: Just drain the ocean.
jokes.setup: Burt Macklin is solving a case involving water
jokes.punchline: His solution is impossibly dramatic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burt Macklin logic - the impossibly over-the-top solution delivered with complete confidence

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Burt Macklin is on the case. He arrives at the water treatment facility at dawn. The morning mist clings to his leather jacket as he surveys the scene. He finds a single drop of water on the ground. He tastes it. His eyes widen. He knows what he has to do.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: He commandeers a helicopter. He flies to the municipal water supply. He rappels down into the main reservoir. With his bare hands, he drains it. Every. Single. Drop.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Three days later, he finds the culprit: a single molecule of impurity, hiding in a water droplet in the neighboring county. Case closed. Burt Macklin, FBI.

jokes.text: I'm a cop! I should get some handcuffs too!
jokes.setup: Andy receives an honorary police badge
jokes.punchline: He immediately wants more police equipment
jokes.explanation: Good Andy escalation - his immediate power trip from getting a fake badge is perfectly in character

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Wait, so I get to keep this? This is mine now?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Do I get a gun? Can I get a gun? I want a gun.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: And a taser. And one of those little flashlights. And a whistle!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Actually, you know what? Just give me the whole belt. I'll wear it everywhere.

jokes.text: He's not. I want to make it clear, he's not.
jokes.setup: Leslie says Andy is now an official police officer
jokes.punchline: The chief's repeated clarification
jokes.explanation: Good authority figure reaction - the chief's concerned clarification is professionally appropriate and funny

dialogue.speaker: Police Chief
dialogue.line: He's a police officer.

dialogue.speaker: Police Chief
dialogue.line: An official police officer.

dialogue.speaker: Police Chief
dialogue.line: Not a security guard. Not a mall cop. A police officer.

jokes.text: President Jimmy Carter stole my guitar?
jokes.setup: Andy sees the initials J.C. on fabric evidence
jokes.punchline: His wild misinterpretation of the clue
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy detective work - jumping to the most absurd conclusion possible from minimal evidence

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: J.C.? Oh my God, it's Jesus Christ. The evidence is Jesus Christ.

jokes.text: Wait. You're Andy. Yeah. I'm Andy. From the information I got I just assumed you'd be like 10 years old.
jokes.setup: John Cena meets the host of Johnny Karate
jokes.punchline: He expected a child host
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity cameo moment - Cena's reasonable assumption about a kids' show host

dialogue.speaker: John Cena
dialogue.line: So you're the host of Johnny Karate?

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Yeah, that's me!

dialogue.speaker: John Cena
dialogue.line: I thought this was a kids' show.

jokes.text: You're going in the dunk tank, bud. I don't wanna go in the dunk tank.
jokes.setup: Andy arrests John Cena for guitar theft
jokes.punchline: The professional wrestler doesn't want to go in a kids' dunk tank
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy setup - a WWE star being dominated by Andy and afraid of a dunk tank

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You're under arrest for guitar theft.

dialogue.speaker: John Cena
dialogue.line: You can't see me going in a kids' dunk tank.

jokes.text: Is anybody in charge? Uh, a little help?
jokes.setup: John Cena being forcibly put in the dunk tank by Andy
jokes.punchline: Professional wrestler asking for help from a children's show host
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup - the role reversal of tough guy John Cena being overpowered by Andy's enthusiasm is inherently funny.

jokes.text: Well, not here, here, because this studio actually has a show where divorced couples work out their problems, so that might be weird if that thing was hanging over them.
jokes.setup: Leslie says Andy's karate gi will stay in the studio forever
jokes.punchline: She explains the awkward reality of shared studio space
jokes.explanation: Great behind-the-scenes humor - the practical reality of local TV production undermining the romantic gesture

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: Yeah, your karate gi is going to stay in this studio forever. It's going to become part of the studio ecosystem. Like, in fifty years, someone's going to find it and be like, 'Whose is this?' and we'll all be dead, so nobody will know.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: That's dark.

dialogue.speaker: Leslie
dialogue.line: That's just the reality of shared studio space.

jokes.text: I once found a rock that looked exactly like Santa Claus. Hat and everything, so...
jokes.setup: Andy says today was the best day of his life
jokes.punchline: His previous best day involved finding a rock
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy moment - his innocent joy over simple things, and the unnecessary detail about the hat

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Today was the best day of my life.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: My previous best day involved finding a rock.

jokes.text: Like, even better than my all-time favorite host, Tom Snyder, which is crazy.
jokes.setup: April is praising Andy's hosting abilities
jokes.punchline: Her unexpected reference to an obscure late-night host
jokes.explanation: Great April character detail - her knowledge of obscure TV history fits her perfectly

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Andy, you're actually a really good host. You should do late-night TV.

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: Really? You think so?

dialogue.speaker: April
dialogue.line: Yeah, you'd be perfect. You have the charm of Conan O'Brien and the intelligence of... Conan O'Brien.

jokes.text: You're my Verizon-Chipotle-Exxon.
jokes.setup: Andy is telling April she gives him energy
jokes.punchline: He references the absurd commercial from earlier
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback - Andy turning a corporate satire into a romantic declaration is sweetly absurd

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: You give me energy. Like that commercial where the guy drank all that orange soda and then he turned into a basketball hoop.

jokes.text: Could somebody get me outta here? Please? I'll help you, John Cena. No.
jokes.setup: John Cena is still stuck in handcuffs with the tarantula loose
jokes.punchline: Andy's immediate rejection of helping his victim
jokes.explanation: Good ending gag - Andy's petty refusal to help his celebrity 'arrest' while the tarantula threatens

dialogue.speaker: John Cena
dialogue.line: Andy, help me! The tarantula is loose!

dialogue.speaker: Andy
dialogue.line: No way, dude. You're on your own.

insight: Season 7's finale builds to 87.5 with 52 jokes in steady rhythm, character comedy sustaining the send-off.
